title: THETA Control Room — Build Hook Delta

changedProjects.lastKnownStatus: Build hook reported latest project state.
changedProjects.nextAction: v0.4.21 — THE TUBE GLOWS, NOT THE MODULE (+ amber glow glitch fixed). AmberHeatGlow was a component layered over the whole AMBER panel painting coral ellipses centred on each tube with radius up to ~2x the tube width; once v0.4.19 narrowed the tube tiles those blobs smeared across the emerald switches and era tiles (the glitch). Replaced with AmberHeatDriver: the heat envelope now drives each TUBE TILE's own lamp (a heat property read by drawC1SelectorTile, pushing lit 0.72 -> 1.0), so a tube driven hard burns brighter and spills light like the real thing. A stage whose emerald switch is OFF is unpowered and stays cold. Heat quantised to 1/20 so the 30Hz envelope reuses cached lamp faces. VERIFY IN ABLETON: drive AMBER hard and watch the TUBE tiles brighten (no coral wash over the panel); switch a stage off and its tube stays cold. NOTE: proc.amberHeat is ONE module-wide envelope gated per stage — per-tube heat taps would need DSP work in AmberModule. OPEN: painted-metal chassis fork.
